# jamfprotect_data_forwarding (Resource)

Manages data forwarding settings in Jamf Protect.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Manage Jamf Protect data forwarding settings.
resource "jamfprotect_data_forwarding" "example" {
  amazon_s3 = {
    enabled                 = true
    encrypt_forwarding_data = true
    bucket_name             = "example-bucket"
    prefix                  = "jamfprotect"
    iam_role                = "example-iam-role"
  }

  microsoft_sentinel = {
    enabled                  = true
    directory_id             = "example"
    application_id           = "example"
    data_collection_endpoint = "https://endpoint.azure.com"
    application_secret_value = "example-secret"

    alerts = {
      enabled                           = true
      data_collection_rule_immutable_id = "example-alerts-rule-id"
      stream_name                       = "example-alerts-stream"
    }

    unified_logs = {
      enabled                           = true
      data_collection_rule_immutable_id = "example-unified-logs-rule-id"
      stream_name                       = "example-unified-logs-stream"
    }

    telemetry_deprecated = {
      enabled = false
    }

    telemetry = {
      enabled                           = true
      data_collection_rule_immutable_id = "example-telemetry-rule-id"
      stream_name                       = "example-telemetry-stream"
    }
  }
}

# Output the CloudFormation template for the Amazon S3 data forwarding configuration.
output "cloud_formation_template" {
  value = jamfprotect_data_forwarding.imported.amazon_s3.cloudformation_template
}

# Export the CloudFormation template for the Amazon S3 data forwarding configuration to a local file.
resource "local_file" "cloud_formation_template" {
  content  = jamfprotect_data_forwarding.imported.amazon_s3.cloudformation_template
  filename = "cloudformation_template.yml"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `amazon_s3` (Attributes) Amazon S3 forwarding settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--amazon_s3))
- `microsoft_sentinel` (Attributes) Microsoft Sentinel (DCR) forwarding settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--microsoft_sentinel))

### Optional

- `timeouts` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The singleton identifier for data forwarding.

<a id="nestedatt--amazon_s3"></a>
### Nested Schema for `amazon_s3`

Required:

- `bucket_name` (String) The Amazon S3 bucket name.
- `prefix` (String) The prefix for Jamf Protect data objects in the bucket.

Optional:

- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether Amazon S3 forwarding is enabled.
- `encrypt_forwarding_data` (Boolean) Whether forwarded data is encrypted.
- `iam_role` (String) The IAM role ARN assumed by Jamf Protect.

Read-Only:

- `cloudformation_template` (String) The CloudFormation template for setting up S3 forwarding.
- `external_id` (String) The external ID for the IAM role trust policy, derived from the organization UUID.


<a id="nestedatt--microsoft_sentinel"></a>
### Nested Schema for `microsoft_sentinel`

Required:

- `alerts` (Attributes) Alerts forwarding settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--microsoft_sentinel--alerts))
- `application_id` (String) The Azure client ID.
- `data_collection_endpoint` (String) The data collection endpoint (must end with .azure.com, .azure.us, .azure.cn, or .azure.de).
- `directory_id` (String) The Azure tenant ID.
- `telemetry` (Attributes) Telemetry forwarding settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--microsoft_sentinel--telemetry))
- `telemetry_deprecated` (Attributes) Telemetry (deprecated) forwarding settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--microsoft_sentinel--telemetry_deprecated))
- `unified_logs` (Attributes) Unified logs forwarding settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--microsoft_sentinel--unified_logs))

Optional:

- `application_secret_value` (String, Sensitive) The Azure client secret value. Only sent on update.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether Microsoft Sentinel forwarding is enabled.

Read-Only:

- `secret_exists` (Boolean) Whether an application secret is configured.

<a id="nestedatt--microsoft_sentinel--alerts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `microsoft_sentinel.alerts`

Optional:

- `data_collection_rule_immutable_id` (String) The data collection rule immutable ID.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the data stream is enabled.
- `stream_name` (String) The stream name in the data collection rule.


<a id="nestedatt--microsoft_sentinel--telemetry"></a>
### Nested Schema for `microsoft_sentinel.telemetry`

Optional:

- `data_collection_rule_immutable_id` (String) The data collection rule immutable ID.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the data stream is enabled.
- `stream_name` (String) The stream name in the data collection rule.


<a id="nestedatt--microsoft_sentinel--telemetry_deprecated"></a>
### Nested Schema for `microsoft_sentinel.telemetry_deprecated`

Optional:

- `data_collection_rule_immutable_id` (String) The data collection rule immutable ID.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the data stream is enabled.
- `stream_name` (String) The stream name in the data collection rule.


<a id="nestedatt--microsoft_sentinel--unified_logs"></a>
### Nested Schema for `microsoft_sentinel.unified_logs`

Optional:

- `data_collection_rule_immutable_id` (String) The data collection rule immutable ID.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the data stream is enabled.
- `stream_name` (String) The stream name in the data collection rule.



<a id="nestedatt--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
- `delete` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.
- `read` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Read operations occur during any refresh or planning operation when refresh is enabled.
- `update` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
# Terraform 1.5+ Import Example
# Import an existing Jamf Protect custom prevent list using the import block.

import {
  to = jamfprotect_data_forwarding.imported
  id = "data_forwarding_singleton"
}

resource "jamfprotect_data_forwarding" "imported" {
  # Configuration will be populated during import
  # After import, run 'terraform plan' to see the current state
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Copyright Jamf Software LLC 2026
# SPDX-License-Identifier: MPL-2.0


terraform import jamfprotect_data_forwarding.imported "data_forwarding_singleton"
```
